_meta.purpose: Top-of-funnel indicator candidates for Jarvis pipeline. Deterministic downstream execution.
_meta.notes: All candidates are implementable with existing video_data. Families ordered by expected signal strength based on prior research.

already_coded_not_tested.description: These candidates already have METRIC_DEFINITIONS and extract_metric code in pipeline.py but have NOT been run through the pipeline as indicators. They should be tested first since they require no code changes.

excluded_null_signals.description: These were tested in prior research and confirmed as null or dead signals. Not proposed.

signals.reason: LLM scores too noisy at n=203

diversity_buckets.name: Speech Pacing (Timed Words)
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Opening pace r=-0.370 vs keep (causal tree). Silence gaps >1.5s predict drops r=-0.73. Speech acceleration r=+0.176 vs retention.

candidates.label: Opening Speech Rate (0-3s)
candidates.description: Words per second in the first 3 seconds — hook density. Research shows optimal ~7.5 words in first 3s.
candidates.research_signal: r=-0.370 vs keep for opening pace

candidates.label: Opening Speech Rate (0-5s)
candidates.description: Words per second in the first 5 seconds — slightly wider hook window.
candidates.research_signal: r=-0.370 vs keep for opening pace

candidates.label: Closing Speech Rate (last 5s)
candidates.description: Words per second in the final 5 seconds — deliberate delivery at climax (slower = better per research).
candidates.research_signal: Slower pace at climax 3.86 w/s vs 4.26 at drops

candidates.label: Speech Rate Q1 (0-25%)
candidates.description: Words per second in the first quarter of the video.

candidates.label: Speech Rate Q4 (75-100%)
candidates.description: Words per second in the final quarter of the video.

candidates.label: Speech Rate Ratio Q4/Q1
candidates.description: Ending pace divided by opening pace — deceleration signal. <1 means slowing down (good for climax).

candidates.label: Speech Acceleration
candidates.description: Slope of speech rate over video timeline — positive = speeding up, negative = slowing down.

candidates.label: Max Silence Gap (seconds)
candidates.description: Longest gap between consecutive words — dead air signal. Gaps >1.5s predict retention drops at r=-0.73.
candidates.formula: max(words[i+1].timestamp - words[i].timestamp for i in range(len-1))
candidates.research_signal: Silence >1.5s predicts drops r=-0.73 (STRONGEST local signal)

candidates.label: Silence Gap Count (>1s)
candidates.description: Number of gaps between words exceeding 1 second — dead air frequency.

candidates.label: Total Silence Percentage
candidates.description: Total silence time (gaps > 0.5s) as fraction of video duration.

candidates.label: Opening Word Latency
candidates.description: Time in seconds before the first word is spoken. Immediate speech = strong hook.

candidates.label: Peak Speech Rate (3s window)
candidates.description: Maximum words/sec in any 3-second sliding window — burst intensity.
candidates.formula: max(count(words in [t, t+3]) / 3 for all t)

candidates.label: Speech Tempo Range
candidates.description: Max speech rate minus min speech rate across 5s windows — dynamic range of delivery.

candidates.label: Average Word Gap
candidates.description: Mean time between consecutive words — overall delivery pace.

candidates.label: Word Density Variance
candidates.description: Variance in words-per-second across 5s windows — pacing consistency.

diversity_buckets.name: Sensory & Technical Language
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Sensory/body words drive retention peaks (+0.06). Technical/material words kill retention (-0.171). 662 words scored in word impact dictionary. Physical language has weight +1.59 in regression.

candidates.label: Sensory Word Density
candidates.description: Fraction of transcript words that are sensory/body words (curious, painful, bigger, stomach, etc). Strongest positive language signal.
candidates.research_signal: Physical/sensory words +0.06 retention, weight +1.59 in regression

candidates.label: Technical Word Density
candidates.description: Fraction of transcript words that are technical/material words (plastic, solid, fiber, carbon, etc). Strongest negative language signal.
candidates.research_signal: Technical words -0.171 retention (3x larger negative effect)

candidates.label: Sensory-to-Technical Ratio
candidates.description: Ratio of sensory words to technical words. Higher = more engaging language balance.

candidates.label: Hook Sensory Word Count
candidates.description: Number of sensory/body words in the hook segment. Sensory opening grabs attention.

candidates.label: Body Sensation Word Percentage
candidates.description: Percentage of words related to body sensations (feel, touch, hurt, taste, smell, burn, etc).

diversity_buckets.name: Pivot & Transition Words
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Pivot/connector words r=+0.26 vs keep (causal tree). 'But', 'however', 'wait' predict keep at r=0.23.

candidates.label: Pivot Word Count
candidates.description: Count of pivot/transition words (but, however, wait, instead, actually, turns out, except, surprisingly). Signals narrative tension.

candidates.label: Pivot Word Density
candidates.description: Pivot words per 100 transcript words — normalized for video length.

candidates.label: Hook Contains Pivot Word
candidates.description: Whether the hook segment contains at least one pivot word (1=yes, 0=no).

diversity_buckets.name: Transcript Vocabulary Features
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Vocab universality r=+0.323 vs retention (causal tree). Simple, common words retain better.

candidates.label: Short Word Ratio (1-3 chars)
candidates.description: Fraction of words with 1-3 characters — simplicity/accessibility signal.

candidates.label: Long Word Ratio (8+ chars)
candidates.description: Fraction of words with 8+ characters — complexity/jargon signal.

candidates.label: Hapax Legomena Ratio
candidates.description: Fraction of words appearing exactly once — vocabulary uniqueness.

candidates.label: First Person Pronoun Ratio
candidates.description: Fraction of words that are first-person pronouns (I, me, my, mine, myself).

candidates.label: Second Person Pronoun Ratio
candidates.description: Fraction of words that are second-person pronouns (you, your, yours). Research shows 'you' hurts keep rate.
candidates.research_signal: Direct address hurts keep rate (debunked as positive)

candidates.label: Action Verb Density
candidates.description: Fraction of words that are action/physical verbs (making, building, testing, breaking, etc).

candidates.label: Superlative Count
candidates.description: Number of superlative words (biggest, most, fastest, strongest, etc) — intensity signal.

candidates.label: Comparison Word Count
candidates.description: Count of comparison words (vs, than, better, worse, more, less) — competitive framing.

diversity_buckets.name: Hook-Specific Features
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Hook word density r=-0.350 (inverted U, optimal 7.5 words in first 3s). Hook-payoff gap r=-0.520.

candidates.label: Hook Speech Rate (words/sec)
candidates.description: Words per second within the hook segment — hook delivery pace.
candidates.research_signal: Hook word density r=-0.350 with inverted-U

candidates.label: Hook Unique Word Ratio
candidates.description: Lexical diversity within the hook segment only.

candidates.label: Hook Action Verb Count
candidates.description: Number of action verbs in the hook — immediate activity signal.

candidates.label: Hook Sentence Count
candidates.description: Number of sentences in the hook segment — hook complexity.

candidates.label: Hook Average Word Length
candidates.description: Average word length in hook — hook vocabulary complexity.

candidates.label: Hook Number Count
candidates.description: Number of numeric tokens in the hook — specificity in opening.

diversity_buckets.name: Narrative Structure Advanced
diversity_buckets.research_basis: 3/4 arc elements = 13M avg vs 0/4 = 1.4M. Segment type and placement matter. Climax position signals story shape.

candidates.label: Segment Duration Variance
candidates.description: Variance in segment durations — pacing uniformity. Low variance = even pacing.

candidates.label: Segment Count Per Minute
candidates.description: Segments per minute of video — structural density normalized by length.

candidates.label: Has Setup Segment
candidates.description: Whether AI identified a Setup segment (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Has Conclusion Segment
candidates.description: Whether AI identified a Conclusion segment (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Unique Segment Type Count
candidates.description: Number of unique segment labels — narrative complexity.

candidates.label: Narrative Arc Completeness
candidates.description: Count of canonical arc elements present: hook, setup, main_point/body, climax/payoff, conclusion. Score 0-5.

candidates.label: Climax in Last 30%
candidates.description: Whether climax starts in the last 30% of the video (1=yes, 0=no). Late climax = better payoff timing.

candidates.label: Last Segment Duration %
candidates.description: Final segment as percentage of total duration — ending weight.

candidates.label: First Segment Duration %
candidates.description: First segment as percentage of total duration — opening weight.

candidates.label: Segment Length Ratio (Max/Min)
candidates.description: Longest segment divided by shortest — pacing imbalance.

candidates.label: Body Segment Count
candidates.description: Number of segments that are not Hook or Conclusion — middle content density.

candidates.label: Hook+Conclusion Combined %
candidates.description: Hook and conclusion combined as percentage of total duration — bookend weight.

diversity_buckets.name: Title & Metadata Advanced
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Title structure largely null in prior research, but specific keywords matter. 'Making' = $24M avg. Duration sweet spot 50-55s.

candidates.label: Title All-Caps Word Count
candidates.description: Number of ALL CAPS words in the title — emphasis/shouting.
candidates.formula: count(words where word.isupper() and len >= 2 in title)

candidates.label: Title Contains Emoji
candidates.description: Whether the title contains any emoji character (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Title Contains 'Making'
candidates.description: Whether the title contains 'making' or 'made' (1=yes, 0=no). Strongest concept keyword.
candidates.formula: int('making' in title.lower() or 'made' in title.lower())

candidates.label: Title Unique Word Ratio
candidates.description: Lexical diversity in the title.

candidates.label: Title Average Word Length
candidates.description: Average word length in the title — title complexity.

candidates.label: Title Starts With Action Verb
candidates.description: Whether the title begins with an action/gerund word (1=yes, 0=no).
candidates.formula: int(title_words[0] ends with 'ing' or is action verb)

candidates.label: Title Length Optimal (3-7 words)
candidates.description: Whether title word count is in the 3-7 word sweet spot.

candidates.label: Description Word Count
candidates.description: Word count of video description — metadata effort signal.

candidates.label: Duration in Sweet Spot (40-55s)
candidates.description: Whether duration falls in the 40-55 second optimal range. 50-55s = 2.88M avg.

candidates.label: Distance from Duration Sweet Spot
candidates.description: Absolute seconds away from 52s sweet spot center. Lower = better.

candidates.label: Is Vertical Video
candidates.description: Whether the video is in vertical format (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Upload Month
candidates.description: Month of upload (1-12) — seasonal effects on algorithm/audience.

diversity_buckets.name: Visual Frame Advanced
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Action frames 28% at peaks vs 8% at worst. Face MORE visible at drops (talking head trap). Workshop settings win. Frame variety paradox: more variety = less retention r=-0.28.

candidates.label: Opening Frame Has Text
candidates.description: Whether the first frame has a text overlay (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Opening Frame Has Face
candidates.description: Whether the first frame shows a face (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Action Frame Percentage
candidates.description: Percentage of frames with action/motion/physical activity. Action enriched 1.9x at retention peaks.
candidates.research_signal: Action frames 28% at peaks vs 8% at drops

candidates.label: Workshop/Tools Frame Percentage
candidates.description: Percentage of frames showing workshop, workbench, or tools — familiar production setting.
candidates.research_signal: Familiar workshop settings win over exotic

candidates.label: Outdoor Frame Percentage
candidates.description: Percentage of frames showing outdoor scenes — outdoor 0.68x at peaks (underrepresented).

candidates.label: Face Without Action Percentage
candidates.description: Frames with face but NO action keywords — talking head trap signal. Face at drops 3.51 vs peaks 3.20.
candidates.formula: count(frames with face AND NOT action) / total_frames
candidates.research_signal: Talking head trap: face visibility higher at drops

candidates.label: Face With Action Percentage
candidates.description: Frames with BOTH face AND action — person doing something (positive signal).
candidates.formula: count(frames with face AND action keywords) / total_frames

candidates.label: Object Focus Percentage
candidates.description: Frames focused on objects/products rather than people.

candidates.label: Text Overlay in First 25%
candidates.description: Text overlay presence in first quarter of frames — early hook text usage.

candidates.label: Cinematography Variety
candidates.description: Number of unique cinematography types across frames (close-up, wide, medium, etc).

candidates.label: Dramatic Frame Percentage
candidates.description: Frames with dramatic/intense visual descriptions. Dramatic enriched 1.8x at peaks.

diversity_buckets.name: AI Analysis Features
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Idea length r=+0.012 in CV model. Concept type matters strongly (indestructible + making = 143.8M).

candidates.label: Video Idea Word Count
candidates.description: Word count of the AI-generated video idea — concept complexity.

candidates.label: Video Idea Character Count
candidates.description: Character count of the video idea.

candidates.label: Summary Word Count
candidates.description: Word count of the AI-generated video summary — content depth.

candidates.label: Video Idea Contains Question
candidates.description: Whether the video idea contains a question mark (1=yes, 0=no).

candidates.label: Video Idea Contains Number
candidates.description: Whether the video idea contains a digit — specificity signal.

candidates.label: Video Idea Contains 'Making'
candidates.description: Whether the idea description contains 'making' or 'build' concept keywords.
candidates.formula: int(any(w in idea.lower() for w in ['making', 'made', 'build', 'built', 'creating']))

candidates.label: Video Idea Contains 'Indestructible'
candidates.description: Whether the idea contains indestructible/unbreakable concept keywords.
candidates.formula: int(any(w in idea.lower() for w in ['indestructible', 'unbreakable', 'bulletproof', 'strongest']))

diversity_buckets.name: Retention Curve Advanced
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Retention at 20s is strongest single predictor r=+0.600. Hook-payoff gap r=-0.520. End recovery 8.5x more important than hook.

candidates.label: Hook-Payoff Gap
candidates.description: Final 5% retention minus hook retention (first 10%). NEGATIVE = over-delivery (ending exceeds hook). Videos where ending exceeds hook get 9x views.

candidates.label: End Recovery Score
candidates.description: Average retention above linear baseline in the 80-95% region. The ending is 8.5x more important than the hook.
candidates.research_signal: End recovery r=+0.506, 8.5x more important than hook

candidates.label: Momentum Zone Length
candidates.description: Longest consecutive run of above-baseline retention — sustained engagement. Consistency beats spikes.

candidates.label: Retention Recovery Count
candidates.description: Number of times the retention curve rises after a drop — resilience signal.
candidates.formula: count(i where curve[i] > curve[i-1] and curve[i-1] < curve[i-2])

candidates.label: Above-Baseline Area
candidates.description: Total area between the retention curve and linear decay — total over-performance.
candidates.formula: sum(max(0, curve[i] - baseline[i]) for i in range(100))

candidates.label: Below-Baseline Area
candidates.description: Total area below the linear decay baseline — total under-performance.
candidates.formula: sum(max(0, baseline[i] - curve[i]) for i in range(100))

candidates.label: Retention Kurtosis
candidates.description: Kurtosis of retention distribution — tail heaviness / peakedness.
candidates.research_signal: Prior CV Δ=-0.01 but different context

candidates.label: Late Drop Severity (after 60%)
candidates.description: Average drop magnitude in the last 40% of the video — fatal late drops signal.
candidates.research_signal: Late drops are fatal: only 3/472 recovered

candidates.label: Retention Concavity
candidates.description: Mean second derivative of retention curve — concave up (decelerating loss) vs concave down.
candidates.formula: mean(curve[i+1] + curve[i-1] - 2*curve[i] for i in range(1,99))

candidates.label: Retention Quartile Spread
candidates.description: Ratio of mean retention in Q4 (75-100%) vs Q1 (0-25%) — how much retention degrades.

candidates.label: Early vs Late Drop Ratio
candidates.description: Drop magnitude in first 30% vs last 30% — where the bleeding happens. Later drops are 2.15x steeper.

diversity_buckets.name: Post-Upload Analytics Advanced
diversity_buckets.research_basis: view_accel_log strongest single predictor Δ+0.048. w2_w1_ratio Δ+0.026. Engagement rates may be circular — test carefully.

candidates.label: Engagement Rate
candidates.description: Total engagements (likes + comments + shares) per 1000 views. WARNING: may be partially circular with views.

candidates.label: Like-to-Comment Ratio
candidates.description: Likes divided by comments — passive vs active engagement balance.

candidates.label: Subscriber vs Non-Sub Retention Gap
candidates.description: Subscriber average percent viewed minus non-subscriber — how much better subs retain.

candidates.label: Retention Variation (raw)
candidates.description: Direct analytics.retentionVariation value — algorithmic retention variability metric.

candidates.label: Average Percent Viewed
candidates.description: Direct analytics.avgPercentViewed — overall completion proxy.

candidates.label: Engaged View Rate
candidates.description: Fraction of total views that were 'engaged views' — quality viewer signal.

candidates.label: Subscriber View Fraction
candidates.description: Fraction of views from subscribers — base audience dependence.

candidates.label: Day 1 View Share
candidates.description: Day 1 views as fraction of first 7 days — initial push strength.

candidates.label: Week 3 / Week 1 Ratio
candidates.description: Sustained performance after initial push — long-tail signal.

candidates.label: Daily Views Entropy
candidates.description: Shannon entropy of daily views distribution — concentrated vs spread views.

candidates.label: Daily Views Gini Coefficient
candidates.description: Gini coefficient of daily views — inequality of view distribution over time.

candidates.label: Stayed to Watch Rate
candidates.description: Direct swipeRatio.stayedToWatch percentage — initial hook conversion.

candidates.label: Average View Duration (seconds)
candidates.description: Direct analytics.avgViewDuration — raw watch time.

diversity_buckets.name: Cross-Layer Interaction Terms
diversity_buckets.research_basis: Interactions found in model: indestructible_x_prev_keep Δ+0.026. Cross-layer interactions can capture non-additive effects between pre-upload content and post-upload performance.

candidates.label: Sensory Density × Final 5% Retention
candidates.description: Language quality × ending retention — do sensory videos end stronger?

candidates.label: Pivot Count × Segment Count
candidates.description: Transition words × structural complexity — narrative density.

candidates.label: Hook Speech Rate × Hook Duration
candidates.description: Hook pace × hook length — hook information density.

candidates.label: Duration × Speech Rate
candidates.description: Length × pace — total word count proxy but as interaction.

candidates.label: Face % × Hand %
candidates.description: Face presence × hand activity — doing vs just talking.
candidates.research_signal: Face+action at peaks, face-alone at drops

candidates.label: Scene Change Rate × Segment Count
candidates.description: Visual pace × narrative pace — editorial density.

candidates.label: Sensory Density × Speech Rate
candidates.description: Sensory language × delivery pace — engaging content delivered well.

candidates.label: Speech Rate × Retention Entropy
candidates.description: Delivery pace × retention complexity — pace drives complex curves?

candidates.label: Hook-Payoff Gap × Retention Entropy
candidates.description: Over-delivery × curve complexity — best endings with rich retention.

candidates.label: Opening Rate × Max Silence
candidates.description: Fast start × worst dead air — delivery consistency.

candidates.label: Arc Completeness × Duration
candidates.description: Story fullness × video length — do longer videos need more arc elements?

candidates.label: Sensory/Technical Ratio × Retention Entropy
candidates.description: Language balance × curve complexity — good language + rich retention.

candidates.label: Action Frames × Speech Rate
candidates.description: Visual action × verbal pace — audiovisual alignment.

candidates.label: Making Concept × Hook Duration %
candidates.description: Strongest concept × hook weight — do making videos benefit from longer hooks?

candidates.label: Silence Gaps × Segment Count
candidates.description: Dead air frequency × structural pieces — pacing breakdown indicator.

candidates.label: Technical Words × Face Alone
candidates.description: Technical language × talking head — double penalty signal.

candidates.label: Pivot Density × Hook Speech Rate
candidates.description: Transition language × hook pace — engaging opening setup.

candidates.label: Closing Speech Rate × End Recovery
candidates.description: Ending pace × ending retention — deliberate ending delivery.
candidates.research_signal: Slower pace at climax + end recovery both strong signals

candidates.label: Making Title × Optimal Duration
candidates.description: Best concept keyword × sweet spot duration.

candidates.label: Vocabulary Diversity × Segment Type Count
candidates.description: Lexical richness × narrative variety.
